title: Smugglers' Alliance

The '''Smugglers' Alliance''' was a coalition of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] that was led by [[Talon Karrde]] to represent their interests to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] so they could sell information, move cargo legitimately and liaise with their sponsor.

==History==
===Background===
After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the members of several illegal organizations worked together in an organization which was referred to as the Smuggler Alliance. Led by Talon Karrde from the [[Car'das/Legends|Car'das smugglers]], its members also included [[Dravis]]<ref name="SWG:JtL">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed]]''</ref> from [[Billey]]'s smugglers,<ref name="TLC" /> [[Shamdon Kree]] from the [[Valarian Syndicate]], [[Beissa]] from [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's criminal empire]] as well as [[Nirame Sakute]] from the [[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]].<ref name="SWG:JtL" /> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> the Smuggler Alliance recruited a number of freelancer pilots who formed a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] that defended interests of the Alliance. This Smuggler Alliance also had ties with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and the Smuggler Alliance pilots sometimes worked for Rebel [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Willham Burke]].<ref name="SWG:JtL" />

===Formation===
[[File:SmugglersAlliance1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Smuggler/Legends|Smugglers]] unite against an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Ambush on Trogan|ambush]].]]
The Smuggler's Alliance found its roots in the loose coalition of top smugglers brought together by Karrde on [[Trogan/Legends|Trogan]] to aid the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] against the assaults of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]].<ref name="TLC" /> After playing a major role in the defeat of Thrawn's forces at the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]], the collaboration coalesced into a formal sponsorship with the New Republic with the help of [[Sian Tevv/Legends|Sian Tevv]], who convinced the [[New Republic Provisional Council]] of the value presented by third-party spacers.

===Growth===
During the height of the [[Imperial Civil War]], Palpatine arranged for the capture of the former Imperial assassin [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], who had left the service of the Empire and cut herself off from the Emperor's telepathic commands after killing a [[Luuke Skywalker|clone]] of Luke Skywalker in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. After Jade was intercepted on a Smuggler's Alliance mission to [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], [[Cronal|Lord Cronal]] had her imprisoned, but she was eventually rescued by New Republic forces under the command of [[Kyle Katarn]], whom she later trained under.<ref name="DFS5">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod}}</ref>

For over fifteen years, the Smugglers' Alliance and the New Republic worked together in a mutually beneficial relationship, with [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] acting as a liaison until she wed [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and resettled on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. The smugglers provided the New Republic with intelligence and vital supplies in conflict zones, while the New Republic provided aid to the smugglers' legal activities within New Republic space and allowed them free reign to smuggle inside hostile space.

===The Yuuzhan Vong invasion===
The [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] drove a spike through an alliance already weakened by the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty]]'s reduction in areas smugglers could operate in without concern. In the early stages of the invasion, smugglers ran supplies, weapons and refugees to and from worlds. As beings began to lose faith in victory against the extragalactic invaders, some smugglers began to join the [[Peace Brigade]] and offer their services to the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] in hopes of earning good grace.

Many, however, saw the Yuuzhan Vong for what they were and sided with the New Republic. A small fleet of smugglers and other scoundrels, including [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] with his [[Lady Luck/Legends|''Lady Luck'']] and [[Booster Terrik]] and his [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Errant Venture/Legends|Errant Venture]]'', banded together to aid the New Republic forces at the [[Battle of Ebaq]] 9. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] led the smuggler fleet from the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], and attacked the massive Yuuzhan Vong fleets with inventive, unorthodox strategies. Although they suffered heavy losses, the smugglers were successful in antagonizing the Yuuzhan Vong, and when the aliens turned towards [[Ebaq 9]] to attack the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and Republic forces hiding within the [[Moon/Legends|moon]], the smugglers threw themselves in their path, delaying their approach and buying crucial time for those hiding to get to fortified positions.

[[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]] saw the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar]] and a major change for the Smugglers' Alliance. The demand for illegal goods hit rock bottom as the short supply of [[Spice/Legends|spice]] forced users to confront their addictions and economies were rattled. In this situation, the leaders of the group claimed that they were turning respectable, and putting their cargo fleets to legitimate use in the rebuilding of Galactic civilization. Karrde joked that the organization was becoming the "Ex-Smuggler's Alliance".

This did not mean that the Alliance ceased to exist, merely that its members seemed about to become ex-smugglers.

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Wikipedia:MMORPG|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', the Smugglers' Alliance is one of three squadrons that freelance pilots may choose to join. This suggests that an early version of the Smugglers' Alliance existed as early as 1 ABY.<ref name="SWG:JtL" />
